q1b5cykz.title_ar: «عقارات» تعتمد مناطق جديدة لتملك وانتفاع غير القطريين بالعقارات
q1b5cykz.full_text:
«عقارات»: مناطق جديدة للتملك والانتفاع بالعقارات لغير القطريين
 اقتصاد
 15 يونيو 2026 , 01:24ص
 سامح الصديق
 أعلنت الهيئة العامة لتنظيم القطاع العقاري «عقارات» اعتماد تحديثات جديدة على الخارطة الاستثمارية في دولة قطر، تتضمن إضافة مناطق جغرافية جديدة تتيح لغير القطريين التملك والانتفاع بالعقارات، في خطوة تستهدف تعزيز البيئة الاستثمارية وتوسيع الفرص المتاحة أمام المستثمرين المحليين والدوليين، بما يدعم النمو العمراني والاقتصادي في الدولة. وأكدت «عقارات» في منشور عبر حسابها الرسمي في منصة «إكس» أن التحديثات الجديدة تأتي في إطار جهودها المستمرة لتطوير القطاع العقاري ورفع جاذبيته، من خلال توفير خيارات استثمارية متنوعة تتماشى مع التطورات التي يشهدها السوق العقاري القطري، وتسهم في استقطاب المزيد من الاستثمارات النوعية إلى مختلف المناطق الواعدة.
ووفقاً للخارطة الاستثمارية المحدثة، تم توسيع نطاق المناطق المخصصة للتملك الحر لغير القطريين لتشمل عدداً من المواقع الإستراتيجية، من بينها مشروع منتجع وشاطئ سميسمة ومنتجع الخور، إضافة إلى مناطق بارزة في لوسيل وجبل ثعيلب والدفنة واللؤلؤة، حيث تتيح هذه المناطق التملك الكامل للعقارات مع إمكانية التصرف بها من خلال البيع أو الشراء أو التوريث دون قيود زمنية.
كما شملت التحديثات تعزيز مناطق الانتفاع طويل الأمد في عدد من المواقع الحيوية، بما يوفر خيارات أوسع للمستثمرين الراغبين في الاستفادة من الفرص العقارية المتاحة في السوق القطرية، سواء للأغراض السكنية أو التجارية أو الاستثمارية.
وتسعى هذه الخطوة إلى دعم مستهدفات التنمية العمرانية المستدامة وتعزيز مساهمة القطاع العقاري في الاقتصاد الوطني، من خلال تحفيز الطلب على المشاريع العقارية وتوسيع قاعدة المستثمرين، فضلاً عن دعم جهود الدولة في تنويع الاقتصاد وجذب رؤوس الأموال الأجنبية. وتأتي التحديثات الجديدة في وقت يواصل فيه القطاع العقاري في دولة قطر تحقيق مستويات متقدمة من النمو والتطور، مدعوماً بالبنية التحتية الحديثة والمشروعات الكبرى والتشريعات الداعمة للاستثمار، بما يعزز مكانة قطر كوجهة عقارية واستثمارية رائدة على المستويين الإقليمي والدولي. وشهد القطاع العقاري في دولة قطر خلال السنوات الأخيرة تطورات تشريعية مهمة عززت من جاذبيته للمستثمرين الأجانب، وفي مقدمتها صدور القانون رقم (16) لسنة 2018 بشأن تنظيم تملك غير القطريين للعقارات والانتفاع بها، والذي أتاح لغير القطريين التملك والانتفاع في مناطق محددة بالدولة وفق ضوابط وتشريعات واضحة.
ويسمح القانون لغير القطريين بالتملك الحر للعقارات في عدد من المناطق الاستثمارية المعتمدة، بما يتيح للمستثمر حق التصرف الكامل في العقار من خلال البيع والشراء والرهن والتوريث دون قيود زمنية، إلى جانب إمكانية الانتفاع بالعقارات في مناطق أخرى لفترات طويلة تصل إلى 99 عاماً.
وأسهم القانون في فتح آفاق جديدة أمام الاستثمار العقاري في قطر، حيث منح المستثمرين الأجانب مزايا متعددة، من بينها الحصول على الإقامة العقارية وفق الشروط المعتمدة، إضافة إلى الاستفادة من البيئة الاستثمارية المستقرة والبنية التحتية المتطورة التي تتمتع بها الدولة.

q1un0wk6.title_ar: «مؤسسة أسباير زون تفتح باب التسجيل لمخيمها الصيفي السنوي»
q1un0wk6.full_text:
Doha: Aspire Zone Foundation has announced that its annual Summer Camp will be held from July 5 to 30, running Sunday through Thursday from 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. at the Aspire Dome for boys and the Ladies Sports Hall for girls.
The Aspire Summer Camp is one of the foundation's flagship annual programs for children. Since its launch in 2017, it has established itself as a comprehensive platform combining sports, educational, and recreational activities for young participants.
The camp is open to boys and girls aged 6 to 14, offering a packed schedule of sports activities, educational workshops, and field trips to some of Doha’s most prominent landmarks.
Registration is now open through Aspire Zone Foundation’s official website.
Commenting on the initiative, Executive Director of Facilities and Events Ahmed Mohammed Al Hassan said Aspire Zone opens its world-class facilities to children from across the community, driven by their strong belief in the importance of instilling a culture of sport and healthy living in younger generations.
Through this camp, he stresses, they aim to help build a healthier, more active, and more aware generation.
The camp will feature a wide range of interactive activities tailored to different age groups and skill levels. Boys’ activities include archery, football, basketball, volleyball, e-sports, swimming, martial arts, and fitness games, while girls will take part in archery, arts activities, swimming, e-sports, basketball, volleyball, and fitness games.
Aspire Zone Foundation also noted that Tuesdays will feature sports and educational programs in cooperation with several organizations, including the Ministry of Endowments and Islamic Affairs, the Qatar Red Crescent Society, the Qatar Sports for All Federation, and the General Directorate of Traffic. Meanwhile, Thursdays will be dedicated to recreational and educational field trips to major attractions across Doha.
The camp will conclude with a closing ceremony on July 30.

q1xats2l.title_ar: ملك البحرين يتسلم رسالة خطية من رئيس قبرص
q1xats2l.full_text:
Manama: Bahrain's King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa has received a written message from Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides concerning bilateral relations between the two countries.
The message was received by Bahraini Foreign Minister Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani during a meeting with Cyprus Ambassador to Bahrain Andreas Eliades.
The meeting also addressed bilateral cooperation and a number of regional and international issues of mutual interest.

q8krgiv.title_ar: «داندي» تبدأ بناء سجل الأوامر تمهيداً لإدراجها في بورصة قطر
q8krgiv.full_text: Dandy Ltd Company has kicked off a book-building process for a 40% stake sale and a planned listing on the Main Market of the Qatar Stock Exchange (QSE), after obtaining approval from the Qatar Financial Markets Authority (QFMA).The company, which has produced dairy, beverage and ice cream products in Qatar for more than 45 years, is looking to price the offer shares between QR5.00 and QR5.20 each, excluding offering and listing fees, according to an institutional announcement on the Dandy website. Dandy’s issued and fully paid-up share capital is QR103mn, divided into 103mn ordinary shares at a nominal value of QR1.00 each, the website stated.The offering is structured in two phases, according to the QSE. Under the first phase, 12.36mn shares representing 30% of the offer will go to institutional investors through book-building. The remaining 28.84mn shares, or 70% of the offer, will be sold to Qatari nationals and Qatar-registered entities at the price determined through book building.Dandy stated that eligible participants in the public subscription include individual Qatari nationals and legal entities holding a valid commercial registration certificate in Qatar, as well as other categories permitted under the applicable rules.The selling price, according to Dandy, will be the book-built figure plus QR0.01 per share in offering and listing fees. Maroon Capital Advisory is the appointed offering and listing adviser, it noted.Participation in the book-building process is confined to QFMA-approved institutional investors and is open by private invitation only through the offering and listing adviser, Dandy stated.The QSE stated that the offering is being conducted under QFMA Board Decision No (8) of 2025 on Offering, Listing, Mergers and Acquisitions Rules, as part of efforts to draw more companies to the capital market.The QSE also noted that the listing reflects collaboration between the QFMA and the exchange to develop the market’s regulatory framework and expand options for companies seeking to list.The bourse explained that the book-building mechanism is used across global and regional markets to allow institutional investors, who have the expertise and tools for fair security pricing, to set the offer price.

qdzdwlq.title_ar: الدبلوماسية القطرية قوة رئيسية وراء الاختراق الأمريكي الإيراني
qdzdwlq.full_text: DohaQatari diplomacy, in close coordination with several international and regional parties, succeeded in narrowing the gaps and bringing the viewpoints closer between the United States of America and the Islamic Republic of Iran, leading to the formulation of initial understandings, culminating in the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the two sides regarding the outstanding issues, including freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, in light of a negotiation process characterized by highly sensitive political and security complexities in the Middle East region.The framework MoU between Washington and Tehran is consistent with the pillars and priorities of the State of Qatar’s foreign policy in terms of adopting political solutions to end disputes and conflicts, instead of wars and military confrontations, which drain material and human resources and hinder development, progress and construction efforts, while always calling for the resolution of disputes through peaceful means and through negotiation and a comprehensive political settlement.The framework MoU between the United States of America and the Islamic Republic of Iran confirms the vitality and centrality of the great diplomatic work carried out by the State of Qatar in regional and international efforts to calm tensions, through an active political movement that combines high-level contacts, consultations and continuous meetings with various relevant parties.The commendable efforts and numerous intensive contacts made by His Highness the Amir of the State of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani with the two parties to the crisis, US President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, along with leaders of fraternal Arab and Islamic countries and foreign countries, are not hidden from anyone. In addition, there was a series of meetings, encounters and foreign visits by Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs HE Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, which reflected an active Qatari presence in managing the complex files of the region, in addition to visits by a number of leaders, ministers and officials and meetings in Doha.The past few weeks have witnessed intense diplomatic activity, including direct contacts between Doha, Washington, Tehran, and Islamabad, the main mediator in the crisis, along with a number of influential capitals. During these contacts, developments in the Middle East region were discussed, along with ways to reduce escalation, support dialogue, and discuss understandings related to reducing and ending tensions and enhancing regional stability, with a focus on ensuring maritime security and the continuity of global supply and energy chains.As efforts to end the crisis between Washington and Tehran accelerated, HH the Amir made a phone call on June 11 to HE US President Trump, during which they reviewed the results of consultations and understandings between the US and Iran, which led to progress in the initial understandings between them.The US president confirmed that these understandings have been approved by the concerned parties, with the participation and support of a number of fraternal countries, including the State of Qatar, while work continues to complete the final procedures in preparation for announcing the arrangements for the aforementioned framework agreement.The Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs also held a phone call on Saturday with Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan Muhammad Shahbaz Sharif, during which they reviewed the relations of cooperation between the two countries and ways to support and strengthen them. They also discussed the latest developments in the region, and Pakistani mediation efforts aimed at reducing escalation in a way that contributes to enhancing security and stability.The PM expressed the State of Qatar’s great satisfaction with the progress made in the negotiations and the announcement by the Pakistani mediation of reaching the final text of the peace agreement, reiterating Qatar’s full support for the Pakistani mediation efforts aimed at ending the crisis through peaceful means.Qatar’s intensive diplomatic efforts since the beginning of the crisis have included a series of contacts with many leaders and officials of fraternal and friendly countries and regional and international leaders, in addition to group, trilateral and bilateral meetings on the sidelines of regional and international conferences, all of which demonstrated extensive Qatari coordination to support the path of de-escalation, and to promote dialogue as a strategic option for resolving the conflict between the US and Iran, and addressing the crises of the region in general, with the continuous emphasis on the need to prioritize political and diplomatic solutions and avoid escalation.Qatar’s role in resolving the current crisis between the American and Iranian sides was not limited to traditional mediation, as it evolved into an integrated diplomatic path based on building trust between the parties, facilitating indirect and direct communication channels, and supporting regional mediation efforts, especially those led by the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, which contributed to providing a more flexible and open negotiating environment for political solutions.In a special statement to Qatar News Agency (QNA), Qatari political analyst Dr Abdullah Al Otaibi emphasized the very important role of the State of Qatar in supporting efforts to reach a framework MoU for peace between the US and Iran, which was led by Pakistan.Dr Al Otaibi stressed that the State of Qatar’s foreign policy is based on mediation in resolving conflicts through peaceful diplomatic means and methods, and has remained keen to support this approach throughout the world and not just in the region, for the benefit of all.He noted that Qatar, with its previous cumulative experience in the field of mediation, successfully helped facilitate peace negotiations between Washington and Tehran, pointing to the great confidence that Qatar enjoys from the international community, given its previous achievements in various negotiation tracks, gaining the trust of the conflicting parties, and its cooperation and partnerships with other sisterly and friendly parties.Dr Al Otaibi explained that the MoU agreement between Washington and Tehran is an understanding and a step in the right direction, and that what is required is to adhere to its terms in order to end the war, move forward to a sustainable peace, and prioritize reason over escalation, which will greatly help the parties to reach a final peace agreement between them.Qatar’s commendable efforts and its good offices to resolve the conflict and disputes between Washington and Tehran have been widely praised and welcomed by the United Nations and several countries, organizations, and regional and international bodies concerned with peace, security, and conflict resolution worldwide.These ongoing diplomatic efforts in mediation and dispute settlement come as an extension of Qatar’s pivotal role, under the guidance of its wise leadership, as a trusted peace broker regionally and globally, a role reflected in historic initiatives and agreements in various parts of the world.The world is also witnessing a growing role by the State of Qatar in mediation and good offices across several areas, including ceasefire agreements, restoration of diplomatic relations, release of hostages, prisoner exchanges, support for national dialogues, resolution of border disputes, and the strengthening of humanitarian efforts at both regional and international levels.Examples of this include, but are not limited to, the Doha Document for Peace in Darfur, Qatar's efforts to promote peace and reconciliation in Lebanon, Gaza, Yemen, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Afghanistan, among other agreements that have led to family reunification and the exchange of the release of prisoners, detainees, and journalists in various parts of the world. This has made Qatar's policy and efforts a unique approach and model of mediation, through its multifaceted strategy and inclusive approach.Since the beginning of the current crisis, the State of Qatar has repeatedly affirmed its full support for all diplomatic avenues aimed at de-escalation and ending the war through diplomatic means. This has strengthened its global standing as a neutral and reliable mediator and contributed to the success of its efforts in building bridges and bringing viewpoints closer between the United States of America and the Islamic Republic of Iran, leading to the formulation of initial understandings.Following the signing of the framework agreement between the United States and the Islamic Republic of Iran, and the commencement of technical talks on its implementation, the international community must work together to ensure its success, so that it may evolve into a final agreement and a sustainable peace pla

q1ep0vwt.title_ar: الأسهم الأمريكية تفتتح على ارتفاع إثر اتفاق أمريكي إيراني
q1ep0vwt.full_text:
New York: Major US stock indexes opened higher on Monday after Washington and Tehran reached a preliminary agreement to end the war in the Middle East and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 162.3 points, or 0.32 percent, to 51,364.55.
The S&P 500 gained 85.3 points, or 1.15 percent, at the open to reach 7,516.75.
The Nasdaq Composite advanced 558.4 points, or 2.16 percent, to 26,447.23.

q1d3116f.title_ar: الكويت تستأنف تشغيلاً محدوداً لشركات الطيران الخليجية والعربية والأجنبية من مبنى T4
q1d3116f.full_text:
محليات
 198
 A+ A-
 الدوحة - قنا
 أعلنت الهيئة العامة للطيران المدني الكويتي استئناف تشغيل محدود لشركات الطيران الخليجية والعربية والأجنبية من خلال مبنى الركاب (T4) في مطار الكويت الدولي.
وقالت الهيئة في بيان لوكالة الأنباء الكويتية (كونا) إن هذا القرار يأتي استشعارا من المسؤولية الوطنية والحرص على خدمة المسافرين ودعم استمرارية أعمال شركات الطيران والحفاظ على انتظام حركة الطيران المدني.
 وأضافت أنه سيتم التنسيق مع شركات الطيران الراغبة بالتشغيل لتحديد مواعيد الرحلات.
وأكدت الهيئة استمرار التنسيق مع مختلف الجهات ذات العلاقة لضمان أعلى مستويات السلامة والأمن وكفاءة التشغيل سائلة المولى عز وجل أن يحفظ الكويت وشعبها وكافة المقيمين على أرضها من كل مكروه.
 اقرأ أيضاً

q1ccaiq5.title_ar: مجلس الشورى يرحب بالاتفاق الأمريكي الإيراني ويقر توصيات لمكافحة سمنة الأطفال
q1ccaiq5.full_text: The Shura Council on Monday welcomed the agreement reached between the United States and the Islamic Republic of Iran on a memorandum of understanding aimed at resolving outstanding issues between the two countries, while also adopting a comprehensive set of recommendations to tackle childhood obesity in Qatar.The Council convened at Tamim bin Hamad Hall at its headquarters under the chairmanship of Speaker HE Hassan bin Abdullah Al Ghanim.At the outset of the session, the Council expressed its support for the US-Iran agreement, particularly its provisions related to guarantees for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.Members voiced hope that the accord would contribute to enhancing regional security and stability, promoting sustainable peace, and serving the interests of peoples across the region and the wider world.The Council also commended the efforts of the State of Qatar, led by His Highness the Amir of the State of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, in fostering dialogue and rapprochement between the parties. It highlighted Qatar’s continued role in supporting peaceful solutions through diplomacy, dialogue, and adherence to international law and the principles of good-neighbourliness.According to the Council, Qatar’s contributions to regional peace and stability have earned broad recognition at both regional and international levels.Following approval of the minutes of its previous session, the Council reviewed a report submitted by the Committee on Health, Public Services and Environment Affairs regarding a public discussion request on childhood obesity in Qatar.Addressing the Council, Al Ghanim described childhood obesity as one of the most pressing health and social challenges facing modern societies, warning that its consequences extend far beyond childhood and can affect individuals throughout their lives.“Childhood obesity represents a genuine threat to the health of future generations due to its physical, psychological, and social impacts,” he said.The Shura speaker emphasized that Qatar’s leadership places significant importance on public health and quality of life within the framework of Qatar National Vision 2030, which seeks to build a society where individuals enjoy both physical and mental well-being.He noted that numerous national strategies, awareness campaigns, and health programmes have already been launched to address the issue, but stressed that continued global growth in obesity rates requires stronger cooperation among all relevant stakeholders.Presenting the committee’s report, HE Abdullah bin Nasser bin Turki Al Subaie, Chairman of the Committee on Health, Public Services and Environment Affairs, said the committee had conducted extensive consultations and meetings with government bodies, experts, and representatives of relevant institutions.The committee examined available studies, reviewed national data, and assessed the underlying causes and consequences of childhood obesity, alongside possible interventions to encourage healthier lifestyles among children.Its findings underscored the need for stronger coordination among institutions, more effective preventive and awareness programmes, increased physical activity, and wider use of digital technologies to support monitoring and behavioural change.After extensive deliberations, the Council approved a recommendation to the Government centered on the creation of a national committee dedicated to combating childhood obesity.The proposed body would be responsible for coordinating national efforts, monitoring the implementation of strategies and programmes, proposing preventive and therapeutic initiatives, tracking performance indicators, and addressing challenges associated with the issue.The Council believes a unified national approach is essential to ensure consistency across sectors and maximize the effectiveness of interventions.Among the recommendations was the introduction of stricter regulations governing the promotion of foods high in sugar, fat, and sodium, particularly those targeting children through digital platforms, media outlets, and food delivery applications.The Council also called for stronger oversight of marketing campaigns directed at young audiences and proposed the establishment of a system for classifying foods according to their nutritional value.Food establishments, restaurants, and delivery companies would be required to provide clear nutritional labels on products marketed to children, while practices encouraging unhealthy consumption would face greater scrutiny.Additionally, the Council recommended regulating the timing of food delivery services to help reduce unhealthy eating habits.Education institutions featured prominently in the Council’s recommendations.Members called for the expansion of physical education and sports programmes in schools, increased opportunities for physical activity through school facilities, and stronger partnerships with sports clubs and youth centres.The recommendations also proposed launching initiatives to reduce the consumption of sugary drinks and unhealthy meals in schools while ensuring healthier alternatives are readily available.Special attention would be given to schools recording higher rates of obesity among students through targeted programmes and interventions.The Council further urged the expansion of health supervision over school canteens and the adoption of unified nutritional standards across educational institutions to guarantee access to balanced and healthy food choices.Recognising the role of technology in promoting healthier lifestyles, the Council recommended establishing an electronic monitoring system linked to a digital platform that would track students’ health indicators and allow parents and relevant authorities to monitor their progress.It also proposed the development of digital applications designed to encourage children to engage in physical activity and adopt healthier habits.Another key recommendation was the creation of a national training programme for school staff focused on nutritional education, behavioural modification, family engagement, and referral mechanisms, enabling educators to play a more active role in obesity prevention.During the session, HE Al Ghanim informed members that a proposal concerning the regulation of wedding ceremonies and the reduction of associated practices had been referred to the Committee on Social Affairs, Labour and Housing for further study and recommendations.The Council also reviewed reports on its participation in the 16th session of the Asian Parliamentary Assembly and related meetings held in Manama earlier this year, as well as its involvement in the 2026 Parliamentary Hearing organised by the Inter-Parliamentary Union at the United Nations headquarters in New York.Concluding the session, Al Ghanim briefed members on his participation in the 39th Conference of the Arab Inter-Parliamentary Union, held virtually under the theme “An Arab Parliamentary Vision for a More Stable and Sustainable Future.”He said his address emphasized the importance of strengthening joint Arab action, enhancing coordination and integration among Arab states, and reinforcing security and stability as essential foundations for sustainable development.The Speaker also stressed the need for continued parliamentary cooperation in defending Arab causes and ensuring their effective representation in regional and international forums.

q1mngnj.title_ar: مجلس الوزراء العماني يرحب بالاتفاق الأمريكي الإيراني ويؤكد على الأمن الإقليمي
q1mngnj.full_text:
Muscat: Oman's Cabinet welcomed the understanding reached between the United States and Iran.
During its meeting, chaired by Sultan Haitham bin Tariq of Oman, the Cabinet expressed appreciation for regional and international efforts, particularly the constructive role played by the Gulf Cooperation Council countries and Pakistan in supporting regional security and stability.
During the meeting, the Sultan of Oman affirmed his country's balanced and neutral role, its support for the principles of justice and law, and its commitment to the safety and security of maritime navigation and freedom of passage in its territorial waters in the Strait of Hormuz, in accordance with international law.
He also stressed the importance of continuing efforts and integrating national and regional action, including joint Gulf action among the GCC states, to mitigate the negative impacts of events unfolding in the region and ensure a high level of preparedness for response and coordination.
